decisions.question: Phase-N Reject contract placement on /prd (Phase 4) and /design (Phase 6) — gate insertion vs replacement, ordering against existing finalization prompts, /design's gate ordering vs the docs/designs/ → docs/designs/current/ directory move
decisions.prd_source: Questions Deferred to Design Q1; R23; AC30a/AC30b/AC30c
decisions.recommended: Option A — Augment Existing Gates. Replace /prd Phase 4 step 4.5 and /design Phase 6 step 6.7's 2-option AskUserQuestion with a 3-option gate (Approve/Reject/Continue-revising); new If-rejected branch parallel to existing approval/iteration branches. Mirrors /strategy Phase 5.2 precedent.

decisions.question: R6 shape-predicate evaluation mechanism — how /scope Phase 1 evaluates the three shape predicates (2+ architectural requirements, references new components, Complex classification) against the just-produced or existing PRD: checklist walk during discovery, structured prompt to the agent, or delegation to a sub-decision skill
decisions.prd_source: Questions Deferred to Design Q2; R6; AC7/AC7b
decisions.recommended: Option A — Structured checklist walk during Phase 1 discovery. Inline walk of PRD's named sections (Requirements, component-noun-phrases, Complexity Assessment) against the three predicates, emits per-predicate verdicts with one-line reasons fed into the chain-proposal output (R7.5). Documented at phase-reference altitude with 3-4 worked examples per predicate.

decisions.question: Parent-to-child signaling mechanism — how /scope tells the child to suppress its own status-aware re-entry prompt on Draft re-entry (across /brief, /prd, /design, /plan). Options: environment variable, argument passthrough, state-file sentinel, topic-slug-only with child's own resume logic, or refuse-and-restart pattern
decisions.prd_source: Questions Deferred to Design Q3 (PLAN-status-aware signaling generalized to all four children); R11 last paragraph; R14 child-isolation rule
decisions.tension_flag: L13 — parent-child API coupling vs R14 child-isolation rule. The suppression mechanism choice trades off against R14 (parent reads only child's durable externally-visible status surface, never internals). Argument-passthrough and env-var approaches couple the parent to the child's input API; state-file sentinel and refuse-and-restart approaches preserve isolation. Cross-validation in Phase 3 must surface this tension explicitly.
decisions.recommended: State-file sentinel via /scope state file's parent_orchestration: block (invoking_child + suppress_status_aware_prompt + rationale), with L13 amended at the pattern-doc layer to permit pattern-level suppression signals as a uniform convention. Child reads parent's state file at well-known path; child $ARGUMENTS surface unchanged; sentinel cleared after child returns. /charter's existing --parent-orchestrated flag migrates to the same mechanism in a follow-up PR.
decisions.cross_validation_note: Recommendation resolves the L13 tension by an explicit pattern-doc amendment (not a per-parent exception). The amended L13 reads: 'Parents do not extend children's input surfaces with parent-specific flags or arguments. A pattern-level suppression signal — defined once in the pattern-doc and used uniformly — is permitted as a parent-orchestration primitive.' Phase 3 must verify this amendment is consistent with D8's pattern-doc edit surface.

decisions.question: Worktree-discipline reference content — the detailed prose for references/parent-skill-worktree-discipline.md: rebase mechanics, 'proceed anyway' state-file recording semantics, integration with the chain-proposal prompt vs Phase 2 child invocation
decisions.prd_source: Questions Deferred to Design Q4; R21; AC28/AC28b
decisions.recommended: Substrate-agnostic top-level reference with four named sections (Trigger Condition, Three-Option Prompt, Recording 'proceed anyway' Divergence, Integration with Chain-Proposal Prompt). Body parent-agnostic; per-parent binding notes in final 'Binding Notes' section naming /scope (v1), /charter (back-edit), /work-on (future).

decisions.question: Cross-boundary state-snapshot semantics on Decision Record write — when /scope writes a re-evaluation Decision Record, does child_snapshots (the per-child status + content-hash dual-check block) advance to record the Decision Record path or stay frozen on the referenced upstream artifact? Has implications for drift detection on subsequent /scope resumes
decisions.prd_source: Questions Deferred to Design Q5; R10 child_snapshots; R11 drift detection
decisions.recommended: child_snapshots STAYS FROZEN on the referenced upstream artifact at the boundary's snapshot moment. Decision Record recorded exclusively in exit_artifacts: (with status Accepted) and in referenced_artifact: (which names the boundary's upstream artifact, not the Decision Record). Downstream children retain prior values or Absent. Drift detection on subsequent /scope resumes compares the live PRD/DESIGN against the boundary snapshot, not against the Decision Record.

decisions.question: Resume-ladder body-slot fills (Slots 5/6/7) for /scope — specific row ordering and prompt vocabulary for the parent-specific body slots: status-aware re-entry across PRD-boundary vs DESIGN-boundary vs PLAN-Draft (with AC17b's DESIGN-above-PRD ordering rule), partial-child-run detection ordering across four children, feeder-doc-detected slot (vacuous in v1 — no feeder per Out-of-Scope #7)
decisions.recommended: Adopt PRD R11's ordering verbatim with slot labels + vocabulary contract. Slot 5 (status-aware re-entry) spans 9 rows in most-downstream-first first-match-wins ordering (PLAN-Active → /work-on; PLAN-Done → /release; PLAN-Draft continue/start-fresh; DESIGN-Accepted three-option Re-evaluate/Revise/Bail at DESIGN-boundary; DESIGN-Proposed continue/start-fresh; PRD-Accepted three-option triad at PRD-boundary; PRD-Draft continue/start-fresh; BRIEF-Accepted auto-skip; BRIEF-Draft continue/start-fresh). Slot 6 (partial-child-run) spans 4 rows (plan/design/prd/brief wip detection most-downstream-first). Slot 7 vacuous in v1 (no feeder); slot is documented as 'No feeder defined; reserved for future').
decisions.ac_traceability: AC17a/AC17b/AC17c literal-substring contracts encoded as a Vocabulary Contract sub-section under Slot 5

decisions.question: Abandonment-forced HTML-comment marker schema — per-artifact-type prose tailored to each child's existing Status section vs a uniform marker; exact marker shape (the literal HTML-comment text); what metadata the marker carries beyond the abandonment flag (triggering_child? partial_phase_reached? timestamp?)
decisions.recommended: Uniform single-line HTML-comment marker (Option A) placed at the END of the existing Status section of each child artifact. Literal text: '<!-- scope-status-block: abandonment-forced; triggering-child: <name>; partial-phase-reached: <phase>; chain-started: <ISO-8601 timestamp> -->'. Four runtime fields populated from /scope state. Whitespace inside comment is significant; do not reorder fields or add line breaks.

decisions.question: Pattern-doc edit surface for the fourth gate type and state-schema extensions — where in references/parent-skill-pattern.md the Mandatory-with-auto-skip entry lands (alongside the existing gate vocabulary or in a new subsection); how boundary: and plan_execution_mode: extend references/parent-skill-state-schema.md while staying I-5-conditional; how references/parent-skill-resume-ladder-template.md gains the refuse-and-redirect meta-ladder rows for PLAN-Active (→ /work-on) and PLAN-Done (→ /release)
decisions.prd_source: R1/R3/R9/R10/R11/R12/R13/R14/R17a/R18/R19 all pattern-level; Decisions 1/3/4/6 in PRD; Downstream Artifacts section
decisions.recommended: Surgical edits across 3 of 4 pattern reference files (~90-112 added lines): (A) parent-skill-pattern.md — NEW 'Gate Vocabulary' section between 'Three Exit Paths' and 'Conditional Feeder Invocation Shape', listing all four gate shapes (EITHER-signal, ALWAYS, shape-dependent, Mandatory-with-auto-skip). (B) parent-skill-state-schema.md — two new conditional-field bullets (boundary:, plan_execution_mode:), Chain-tracking paragraph addition, two replaced R9 paragraphs naming boundary: in BOTH Part 2 (sub-shape) AND Part 3 (I-5 absent-when-ungated). (C) parent-skill-resume-ladder-template.md — single paragraph appended to Slot 5 spec explicating refuse-and-redirect prompt shape (Active → /work-on, Done → /release; 9-row meta-ladder count preserved). (D) parent-skill-child-inspection.md — ZERO edits (audit's verbatim call holds; tactical-chain children all doc-emitting, slot into existing row; PLAN's GH milestone side-effect is not a child-inspection surface).
decisions.ac_traceability: All 11 pattern-level R-numbers (R1, R3, R9, R10, R11, R12, R13, R14, R17a, R18, R19) explicitly covered by name; charter back-edit cost bounded to a reference-table citation addition

cross_validation_findings: All 8 decisions cross-validated. One additive merge surfaced: D3's L13 amendment extends D8's parent-skill-pattern.md edit surface (D8 adds new Gate Vocabulary section; D3 adds L13 amendment paragraph in the existing 'Parents do not extend children's input surfaces' prose). Resolved in Decision Outcome as 'Pattern-doc edit (parent-skill-pattern.md) = new Gate Vocabulary section + L13 amendment, both treated as one logical pattern-doc edit unit'. No conflict requiring researcher restart. All 8 assumption sets internally consistent.

meta.decision_to_proceed_as_one_doc: Confirmed by team-lead 2026-05-25: proceed as one design doc. Splitting an 8-question pattern-design would invert L9 traceability; precedent is DESIGN-shirabe-progression-authoring.md (also 8 decisions).
meta.researchers_in_flight: Spawned 2026-05-25T21:11Z by team-lead: decision-researcher-1 through decision-researcher-8. Each will write to its report_path and SendMessage team-lead on completion. Team-lead shuts down each researcher after its report lands.
couplings_already_merged: Original D1 (/prd Phase-N Reject) + D2 (/design Phase-N Reject) merged into D1 (they share most dimensions; both children grow the same contract surface).
Original D4 (PLAN-status-aware signaling) + D10 (general child-signal mechanism) merged into D3 (the suppression mechanism generalizes across all four children).
Original D9 (validator pass-through cadence) dropped — settled by PRD Decision 10 (strict pass-through at each boundary).
Original D7 (PRD-vs-DESIGN ordering when both exist) folded into D6 (it is a slot-5 row-ordering detail of the resume ladder).
